---
title: rmarkdown + knitr
---

```{knitr_config, include=FALSE}
source("~/github/how2/_knitr_config.R")
```

*********************************************************************

rmarkdown:  

* https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/  
* https://dr-harper.github.io/rmarkdown-cookbook/  
* https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/  
* https://rmarkdown.rstudio.com/lesson-1.html  
* https://www.rstudio.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/03/rmarkdown-reference.pdf  
* https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/  

knitr:  

* https://yihui.name/knitr/  
* https://yihui.name/knitr/demo/graphics/  
* https://kbroman.org/knitr_knutshell/  

misc:  

* https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/kableExtra/vignettes/awesome_table_in_html.html  

*********************************************************************

## css styling

### yaml

```
output:
  html_document:
    css: style.css
```

### css chunk

These can be placed anywhere in the Rmd file, including above the yaml header.

<pre><code>```{css, eval=FALSE}
# make all chunk output wider
pre {
  max-height: 300px;
  float: left;
  width: 500px;
  overflow-y: auto;
}

pre.r {
  max-height: none;
}
```</code></pre>

### out.extra

<pre><code>```{r, out.extra='style="background-color: #9ecff7; padding:10px; display: inline-block;'}
code
```</code></pre>

### class

* http://zevross.com/blog/2017/06/19/tips-and-tricks-for-working-with-images-and-figures-in-r-markdown-documents/

<pre><code>```{css}
# horizontal scroll chunk output
.scroll {
  max-height: 300px;
  float: left;
  width: 910px;
  overflow-y: auto;
}
```</code></pre>

<pre><code>```{r, class.output = "scroll"}
print(mtcars)
```</code></pre>

*********************************************************************

## images

`![](path/to/smallorb.png)` (markdown)

`knitr::include_graphics(img1_path_or_url)`

(HTML)

*TODO*

*********************************************************************

## links

### sections 

`# Missingness {#missingness}`

`(see [Missingness](#missingness) section)`

### tables and figures

*TODO*

*********************************************************************

## reusing code

### ref.label

E.g. evaluate a code chunk early on in the document but don't echo it, and then echo the same chunk later on but without evaluating it.

<pre><code>```{r chunk1, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE}`r ''`
some fancy code
```

blah blah ...

```{r, ref.label="chunk1", eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}`r ''`
```</code></pre>


E.g. put all code in appendices

<pre><code># Appendix: R code {-}

```{r ref.label=knitr::all_labels(engine="R"), echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE}`r ''`
```

# Appendix: python code {-}

```{python ref.label=knitr::all_labels(engine="python"), echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE}`r ''`
```</code></pre>

### code

E.g. echo an external R script without evaluating it.

<pre><code>```{r, code=readLines("myscript.R", warn=FALSE), echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE}`r ''`
```</code></pre>

E.g. eval an external CSS script without echoing it.

<pre><code>```{css, code=readLines("style.css", warn=FALSE), eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE}`r ''`
```</code></pre>


### child

* https://stackoverflow.com/questions/25824795/how-to-combine-two-rmarkdown-rmd-files-into-a-single-output

<pre><code>---  
title: My Report  
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: yes 
---

```{r child = 'chapter1.Rmd'}`r ''`
```

```{r child = 'chapter2.Rmd'}`r ''`
```
</code></pre>

*chapter1.Rmd*

<pre><code>
# Chapter 1

This is chapter 1.
</code></pre>

*chapter2.Rmd*

<pre><code>
# Chapter 2

This is chapter 2.
</code></pre>

*********************************************************************

## verbatim chunks

use this prefix at the start of the chunk

```
<pre><code>
```

and this suffix at the end of the chunk

```
</code></pre>
```

if necessary, add this to the rhs of the closing curly brace at the start of the chunk

```{r, echo=FALSE, comment=""}
cat("`r ''``")
```

*********************************************************************

## tabbed section {.tabset}

<pre><code>## tabbed section {.tabset}

### tab 1

some stuff

### tab 2

some more stuff
</code></pre>

### tab 1

some stuff

*********************************************************************

### tab 2

some more stuff

*********************************************************************

## DT::datatable

*TODO*

*********************************************************************

## Appendix: example configuration chunk {-}

> It is recommended to create a knit configuration chunk as the first chunk in a script with cache = FALSE and include = FALSE options set. 

```{r, code=readLines(system.file("rmarkdown/templates/html_template/resources/html_template_knitr_config.R", package = "oddments"), warn = FALSE), eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
```
